name: dark-cinematic-lab
genre: The contemporary AI-lab landing page: dark cinematic minimalism with terminal affordances. One image carries all emotion; the UI layer stays typographic, hairline, and flat.

provenance.evidence_note: Low visual complexity plus high prototypicality wins the 50ms first impression (style.md). This profile is deliberately prototypical within its genre; the single cinematic image carries the controlled complexity. Dark ground trades broad-audience warmth for in-group developer credibility; treat as an audience parameter, not a universal.

ground-0.role: page ground; near-black with a green-gray cast, never pure #000

ground-1.role: raised panel ground: terminal box, hover fills

ground-2.role: second raise step; active states; use sparingly

ink.role: primary text and focus ring; warm off-white, never pure #FFF for body text

muted.role: secondary text, microlabel default, slash separators, numbers

hairline.role: decorative 1px borders: boxed nav items, grid cell walls, section rules. Decorative only; carries no meaning alone, so it is exempt from the 3:1 UI floor

edge.role: interactive border: outlined buttons and any stroke that must read as a control; holds the 3:1 UI-component floor on every ground

cta.role: THE one filled surface in the system. Nothing else on the page may be filled. CTA emphasis comes from contrast against the ground, not from hue (patterns.md, isolation effect)

scene.role: cold blue-family accent family confined to the generated hero scene; blue is the lowest-risk trust accent (color.md); never chartreuse or dark yellow anywhere

mono_microlabel.stack: ui-monospace, 'SF Mono', SFMono-Regular, Menlo, Consolas, 'Liberation Mono', monospace
mono_microlabel.role: carries ALL wayfinding: ticker, nav, eyebrows, tags, counts, footer. If it navigates or labels, it is mono, uppercase, and tracked

terminal.stack: ui-monospace, 'SF Mono', SFMono-Regular, Menlo, Consolas, 'Liberation Mono', monospace
terminal.role: literal shell text; never tracked, never uppercased; the command must be copyable and real

display_humanist.stack: Seravek, 'Gill Sans Nova', Ubuntu, Calibri, 'DejaVu Sans', source-sans-pro, sans-serif
display_humanist.case: sentence case, quiet confidence, no exclamation
display_humanist.role: the hero headline ONLY; the display face appears nowhere else on the page

body_humanist.stack: Seravek, 'Gill Sans Nova', Ubuntu, Calibri, 'DejaVu Sans', source-sans-pro, sans-serif

italic_contrast.stack: Didot, 'Bodoni MT', 'Noto Serif Display', 'URW Palladio L', P052, Sylfaen, Georgia, serif
italic_contrast.role: optional; the second word of the wordmark and at most one partner wordmark; a single serif grace note against the sans system

layout_grammar.note: Ordered top to bottom. Slot names are the section ids. Nav numbering equals document order of the numbered targets: structure as information.

slots.name: announcement-ticker
slots.contract: One static mono line on ground-0 with a trailing external-link arrow. News as quiet ticker, not banner: no marquee, no dismiss button, no fill, bottom hairline.

slots.name: numbered-nav
slots.contract: Left: geometric mark + caps wordmark + italic contrast word. Center: at most 4 primary destinations in hairline boxes, uppercase mono, each suffixed with a muted two-digit order number (01..04) matching document order. Right: mono utilities, then exactly one filled CTA. At phone width the utilities drop, the destinations wrap to a second visible row: never a hamburger, never more than 5 visible items.

slots.name: cinematic-hero
slots.contract: The single cinematic image slot of the page: a full-bleed generated scene (gradient/SVG, no external assets) doing all emotional work, nature-meets-artifact metaphor. Copy overlays a bottom scrim: mono eyebrow, humanist sentence-case headline, one two-line paragraph, one outlined button plus one plain arrow link. The filled CTA lives in the nav, so the hero adds no second fill.

slots.name: terminal-affordance
slots.contract: A literal shell line in a hairline ground-1 box with a block cursor: $ pip install <package>. Developer-nativeness proven, not claimed. The command must be real or realistically installable; the box gets overflow-x auto.

slots.name: named-backers-strip
slots.contract: Mono BACKED BY label, then backer names as plain text separated by muted slashes: funds and individuals, no logos, no links, no images.

slots.name: case-study-grid
slots.contract: A 1px-gap hairline grid (cells share walls) of partner cells: mono CASE STUDY tag, a typographic partner wordmark (each in a distinct treatment from the system's faces), mono READ MORE arrow. Whole cell is the link. Header row carries the section number and an honest cell count.

slots.name: footer
slots.contract: Top hairline. Brand column with mark, wordmark, one-line mission. Up to three mono link columns with microlabel headers. Bottom hairline row: copyright left, legal links right. Every anchor has an explicit color.

assertions.note: The sample's genre rules restated as machine-checkable tests, plus the hard floors. Every assertion is phrased so a script or audit can pass or fail it.

rules.assert: filled elements per viewport <= 1; count of elements with an opaque non-ground background that are interactive or decorative chrome == 1 for the whole page (the CTA)
every tokens.grounds value has relative luminance <= 0.012 and is not #000000
body text color is not #FFFFFF and contrast(ink, every ground) >= 4.5
every nav label, eyebrow, tag, and footer link computes to a monospace family with text-transform uppercase and letter-spacing >= 0.10em
count of elements using the display size/weight role outside the hero headline == 0
cinematic image slots per page == 1; all other sections contain zero raster or scenic content
every numeric badge binds to a countable fact: nav numbers equal document order of their targets; grid header count equals rendered cell count; role counts bind to a live source, never decoration
page contains >= 1 literal executable command line AND >= 4 named backers AND >= 3 named customer cells
computed box-shadow == none on all elements; border-radius <= 2px on all boxes; decorative border-width == 1px
primary nav destinations <= 5; hamburger/disclosure menus == 0 at every width including 390px
document.scrollingElement.scrollWidth <= 390 at a true 390px viewport (measured inside an iframe host, not a clamped headless window)
contrast >= 4.5:1 for all text; >= 3:1 for interactive borders and meaningful graphics
no ground or accent token falls in the chartreuse/dark-yellow hue band (hue 60-90 with lightness < 60%)
external network requests == 0; @font-face count == 0; all font-family values are system stacks
every interactive element paints a visible outline on :focus-visible with contrast >= 3:1 against its surroundings

page-transition loader appears only after >= 300ms of pending navigation; it is a single centered indeterminate ring on the page ground; under prefers-reduced-motion it is a static mark; it is visibly populated in every animation phase; loaders shown < 300ms == 0
under prefers-reduced-motion: reduce, running animations == 0 and all content renders
with scripts stripped, all page content is visible and anchors still navigate
count of <a> elements without an explicit color rule in scope == 0
count of U+2014 characters in every shipped file == 0
